히브리서 11:1.content: Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.

히브리서 11:2.content: For by this, the elders obtained testimony.

히브리서 11:3.content: By faith, we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible.

히브리서 11:4.content: By faith, Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, through which he had testimony given to him that he was righteous, God testifying with respect to his gifts; and through it he, being dead, still speaks.

히브리서 11:5.content: By faith, Enoch was taken away, so that he wouldn’t see death, and he was not found, because God translated him. For he has had testimony given to him that before his translation he had been well pleasing to God.

히브리서 11:6.content: Without faith it is impossible to be well pleasing to him, for he who comes to God must believe that he exists, and that he is a rewarder of those who seek him.

히브리서 11:7.content: By faith, Noah, being warned about things not yet seen, moved with godly fear, prepared a ship for the saving of his house, through which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is according to faith.

히브리서 11:8.content: By faith, Abraham, when he was called, obeyed to go out to the place which he was to receive for an inheritance. He went out, not knowing where he went.

히브리서 11:9.content: By faith, he lived as an alien in the land of promise, as in a land not his own, dwelling in tents with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise.

히브리서 11:10.content: For he looked for the city which has the foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

히브리서 11:11.content: By faith, even Sarah herself received power to conceive, and she bore a child when she was past age, since she counted him faithful who had promised.

히브리서 11:12.content: Therefore as many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as innumerable as the sand which is by the sea shore, were fathered by one man, and him as good as dead.

히브리서 11:13.content: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and embraced them from afar, and having confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

히브리서 11:14.content: For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own.

히브리서 11:15.content: If indeed they had been thinking of that country from which they went out, they would have had enough time to return.

히브리서 11:16.content: But now they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed of them, to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.

히브리서 11:17.content: By faith, Abraham, being tested, offered up Isaac. Yes, he who had gladly received the promises was offering up his one and only son,

히브리서 11:18.content: to whom it was said, “Your offspring will be accounted as from Isaac,”

히브리서 11:19.content: concluding that God is able to raise up even from the dead. Figuratively speaking, he also did receive him back from the dead.

히브리서 11:20.content: By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau, even concerning things to come.

히브리서 11:21.content: By faith, Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.

히브리서 11:22.content: By faith, Joseph, when his end was near, made mention of the departure of the children of Israel, and gave instructions concerning his bones.

히브리서 11:23.content: By faith, Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.

히브리서 11:24.content: By faith, Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter,

히브리서 11:25.content: choosing rather to share ill treatment with God’s people than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a time,

히브리서 11:26.content: considering the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he looked to the reward.

히브리서 11:27.content: By faith, he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king; for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

히브리서 11:28.content: By faith, he kept the Passover, and the sprinkling of the blood, that the destroyer of the firstborn should not touch them.

히브리서 11:29.content: By faith, they passed through the Red Sea as on dry land. When the Egyptians tried to do so, they were swallowed up.

히브리서 11:30.content: By faith, the walls of Jericho fell down, after they had been encircled for seven days.

히브리서 11:31.content: By faith, Rahab the prostitute didn’t perish with those who were disobedient, having received the spies in peace.

히브리서 11:32.content: What more shall I say? For the time would fail me if I told of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets,

히브리서 11:33.content: who through faith subdued kingdoms, worked out righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,

히브리서 11:34.content: quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, from weakness were made strong, grew mighty in war, and caused foreign armies to flee.

히브리서 11:35.content: Women received their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured, not accepting their deliverance, that they might obtain a better resurrection.

히브리서 11:36.content: Others were tried by mocking and scourging, yes, moreover by bonds and imprisonment.

히브리서 11:37.content: They were stoned. They were sawn apart. They were tempted. They were slain with the sword. They went around in sheep skins and in goat skins; being destitute, afflicted, ill-treated—

히브리서 11:38.content: of whom the world was not worthy—wandering in deserts, mountains, caves, and the holes of the earth.

히브리서 11:39.content: These all, having had testimony given to them through their faith, didn’t receive the promise,

히브리서 11:40.content: God having provided some better thing concerning us, so that apart from us they should not be made perfect.
